title: haemanthus tristis
intro: Haemanthus tristis, also known as the blood lily, is a toxic plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its red to orange-red flowers and red sap. The plant contains toxic compounds such as haemanthamin and homohaemanthamin, which have medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever and inflammation. The health benefits of Haemanthus tristis include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as rheumatism. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by drying the flowers and leaves, then using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although caution should be taken due to the plant's toxicity.
